intro: New York City's parks system traces to an 1856 commission created solely to build Central Park; the 1870 Tweed Charter established a citywide Department of Public Parks with jurisdiction over all of Manhattan's parkland, the lineage from which today's Department of Parks and Recreation descends. NYC Parks now stewards more than 30,000 acres — about 14 percent of the city's land — including over 5,000 individual properties such as playgrounds, athletic fields, pools, and historic monuments across the five boroughs.
